3.happening: Start an empty list of recursion-tree nodes.
3.why: We record each call as a node so the tree can be drawn.
3.invariant: nodes holds every call made so far.

4.happening: Start a lookup from node id to node object.
4.why: Children link to parents by id.
4.invariant: by_id[k] is the node with id k.

5.why: It records each call as active, returned, or a leaf — driving the colors.

6.happening: Explore from stair 0 by calling dfs.
6.why: Every distinct climb is a path from stair 0 that lands on n.
6.invariant: This single call unfolds the entire tree.

7.happening: Return the total number of distinct ways: {answer}.
7.why: It equals the number of paths that reach the top exactly.

10.happening: This call is at stair {i}; its node id is the next free slot.
10.why: Each call gets a unique node in the tree.

11.happening: Create a node labelled with stair {i}.
11.why: The label shows which stair this call is standing on.
11.invariant: A fresh node starts with no children.

12.happening: Add the node to the tree — it pops in on screen.
12.why: The tree unfolds one call at a time, depth-first.

13.happening: Register the node under its id.
13.why: So a child can link back to it.
13.invariant: by_id maps every id to its node.

14.happening: Mark this call active — its frame is on the call stack.
14.why: The amber path from the root shows the live call stack.

15.happening: This call has a parent — link it into the tree.
15.why: Every call except the first is a child of the call that made it.
15.invariant: The tree stays connected as it grows.

15.happening: This is the very first call (stair 0) — it has no parent.
15.why: The root of the recursion tree.
15.invariant: The root has no incoming edge.

16.happening: This call came from climbing 1 step — the left branch.
16.why: dfs(i+1) is the parent's first move.

16.happening: This call came from climbing 2 steps — the right branch.
16.why: dfs(i+2) is the parent's second move.

17.happening: Link this node as the parent's left child.
17.why: It represents climbing 1 step from the parent's stair.
17.invariant: The +1 branch now has an edge.

19.happening: Link this node as the parent's right child.
19.why: It represents climbing 2 steps from the parent's stair.
19.invariant: The +2 branch now has an edge.

20.happening: Stair {i} is exactly the top ({n}) — a complete way!
20.why: Landing on n means this sequence of moves reaches the top.

20.happening: Stair {i} is not the top yet.
20.why: We must keep climbing to reach n.
20.invariant: Only i == n counts as a way.

21.happening: Tag stair {i} a success leaf — one valid way.
21.why: It turns green to mark a complete climb.
21.invariant: Green leaves are the ways being counted.

22.why: It adds one to the caller's running total.

23.happening: Stair {i} is past the top ({n}) — a dead end.
23.why: A +2 move can jump over the top; that path is invalid.

23.happening: Stair {i} is still at or below the top.
23.why: There is still room to keep climbing.
23.invariant: Valid calls satisfy i < n here.

24.happening: Tag stair {i} a dead end — it overshot the top.
24.why: It turns red to mark an invalid path.

25.happening: Return 0 — overshooting is not a valid climb.
25.why: It adds nothing to the caller's total.

26.happening: From stair {i}, try climbing 1 step: recurse to {i+1}.
26.invariant: The left subtree is fully explored before the right.

27.happening: From stair {i}, try climbing 2 steps: recurse to {i+2}.
27.why: Exploring the +2 branch after the +1 branch.
27.invariant: Both branches are explored from this stair.

28.happening: Ways from stair {i} = ways via +1 ({left_val}) + ways via +2 ({right_val}).
28.why: Every path from here starts with a 1-step or a 2-step move.

29.happening: Mark stair {i}'s call finished — it turns blue.
29.why: Both branches are counted; the frame is about to pop.

30.happening: Return {total} ways from stair {i} to the caller.
30.why: The parent adds this into its own total.
30.invariant: Each call reports its subtree's count.

init.happening: Setting up empty structures for the recursion tree.
init.why: We record each call as a node to visualize it.

recurse.happening: Making a recursive call — descending into a subtree.
recurse.why: Each stair branches into a +1 and a +2 move.
recurse.invariant: The call stack traces the current path.

check.happening: Testing whether this stair is the top, past it, or linking it in.
check.why: Leaves stop the recursion; internal calls keep branching.
check.invariant: Only i == n counts as a valid way.

update.happening: Recording a node, its links, or its result.
update.why: State drives the node colors and the running count.
update.invariant: The tree reflects every call made.

return.happening: Returning a subtree's count up to the caller.
return.why: Totals bubble up from leaves to the root.
return.invariant: The root's total is the answer.
